name: Wall Slide
cues: Squeeze shoulder blades down and back
Slide pinky side of hand up the wall
Keep ribs down â€” don't arch your back

compensation_patterns.name: scapular_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulder landmark y decreases relative to ear landmark y by >2cm during flexion

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: hip landmark moves anterior >3cm during overhead reach

name: Supine Shoulder Flexion
cues: Keep your elbow straight
Lift arm overhead slowly, as far as comfortable
Lower with control â€” don't let gravity do the work

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: elbow flexion exceeds 20 degrees during movement

regression: Use opposite hand to assist the movement

name: Pendulum Exercise
cues: Lean forward and let your arm hang
Make small circles with your hand
Let gravity and momentum do the work â€” stay relaxed

compensation_patterns.name: active_muscle_contraction
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulder elevation detected during swing

name: Sidelying External Rotation
cues: Keep elbow pinned to your side
Rotate forearm toward ceiling
Control the lowering â€” don't let the weight drop

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_drift
compensation_patterns.detection: elbow moves away from torso during rotation

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk rotates backward during external rotation

name: Standing Shoulder Abduction
cues: Lift arm out to the side, thumb pointing up
Stop at shoulder height
Lower slowly with control

compensation_patterns.name: scapular_hiking
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulder elevates toward ear during abduction

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lateral_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans away from lifting arm

name: Prone Y-Raise
cues: Lift arms into a Y shape overhead
Squeeze shoulder blades together and down
Keep thumbs pointing to ceiling

compensation_patterns.name: scapular_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders shrug toward ears during lift

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: excessive low back arch during arm lift

name: Internal Rotation with Band
cues: Keep elbow glued to your side
Pull band across your body
Return slowly â€” don't let the band snap back

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_drift
compensation_patterns.detection: elbow moves away from torso

name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
cues: Pull arm across body with opposite hand
Hold for 30 seconds
You should feel a stretch in the back of your shoulder

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: body rotates toward the stretch

name: Doorway Pec Stretch
cues: Place forearm on doorframe at shoulder height
Step forward until you feel a stretch across your chest
Keep your shoulder blade back â€” don't let it round forward

compensation_patterns.name: scapular_protraction
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulder blade rounds forward during stretch

name: Isometric External Rotation
cues: Stand with elbow bent 90 degrees at your side
Press the back of your hand into the wall
Hold for 10 seconds at 50% effort

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_drift
compensation_patterns.detection: elbow moves away from torso during hold

name: Shoulder Shrugs
cues: Lift shoulders straight up toward ears
Squeeze at the top for 2 seconds
Lower slowly and completely

compensation_patterns.name: neck_forward_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: head moves forward during shrug

name: Prone T-Raise
cues: Lie face down and lift arms out to sides forming a T
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top
Keep thumbs pointing toward ceiling

compensation_patterns.name: scapular_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders shrug toward ears during lift

compensation_patterns.name: cervical_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: head lifts excessively during arm raise

regression: Reduce range of motion, keep arms lower

name: Prone W-Raise
cues: Lie face down with elbows bent to form a W shape
Lift arms while squeezing shoulder blades down
Rotate forearms so thumbs point to ceiling

compensation_patterns.name: scapular_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders rise toward ears during lift

regression: Perform without lifting arms, just squeeze

name: 90/90 External Rotation with Band
cues: Hold arm at 90 degrees abduction with elbow bent 90
Rotate forearm from parallel-to-floor to perpendicular
Keep elbow at shoulder height throughout

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_drop
compensation_patterns.detection: elbow drops below shoulder level during rotation

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk arches backward during external rotation

name: Seated Overhead Press
cues: Press dumbbells overhead until arms are nearly straight
Keep core engaged, do not arch your back
Lower to ear level with control

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches excessively during press

compensation_patterns.name: asymmetric_press
compensation_patterns.detection: one arm extends significantly ahead of the other

regression: Reduce weight or press to forehead height only

name: Scapular Push-Up
cues: Start in push-up position with arms straight
Without bending elbows, push shoulder blades apart
Then let shoulder blades come together â€” feel the squeeze

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: elbows flex during the movement

compensation_patterns.name: hip_sag
compensation_patterns.detection: hips drop below shoulder-ankle line

regression: Perform on knees or against wall
progression: Add a push-up between each scapular rep

name: Standing External Rotation with Band
cues: Keep elbow tucked at your side with a towel roll
Pull band outward rotating forearm away from body
Return slowly â€” control the band

compensation_patterns.name: elbow_drift
compensation_patterns.detection: elbow lifts away from side during rotation

compensation_patterns.name: wrist_deviation
compensation_patterns.detection: wrist bends instead of forearm rotating

progression: Use heavier band or add towel roll for more ROM

name: Wall Angels
cues: Stand with back flat against wall, arms in goalpost position
Slide arms up the wall keeping wrists and elbows in contact
Go only as high as you can while maintaining wall contact

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back loses contact with wall during overhead reach

compensation_patterns.name: arm_lift_off
compensation_patterns.detection: wrists or elbows lose contact with wall

regression: Reduce range â€” stay below shoulder height

name: Sleeper Stretch
cues: Lie on your affected side with arm at 90 degrees
Use opposite hand to gently push forearm toward floor
Stop when you feel a comfortable stretch in the back of your shoulder

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_roll
compensation_patterns.detection: body rolls backward away from the stretch

name: Quad Set
cues: Sit with leg straight, push the back of your knee into the mat
Tighten your thigh muscle â€” try to lift your heel slightly
Hold for 10 seconds, then relax

compensation_patterns.name: hip_flexor_substitution
compensation_patterns.detection: hip flexion occurs instead of isolated quad contraction

regression: Place towel roll under knee for slight flexion

name: Supine Straight Leg Raise
cues: Tighten your quad first, then lift the whole leg
Keep your knee completely straight
Lift to about 45 degrees, then lower slowly

compensation_patterns.name: knee_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: knee flexion exceeds 10 degrees during lift

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis tilts or rotates during leg raise

name: Sidelying Straight Leg Raise
cues: Lie on your side with legs straight and stacked
Lift top leg toward ceiling, keeping knee straight
Lower with control â€” don't drop it

compensation_patterns.name: hip_flexion_substitution
compensation_patterns.detection: leg moves forward instead of straight up during abduction

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_roll
compensation_patterns.detection: body rolls backward during lift

name: Heel Slides
cues: Lie on your back and slide your heel toward your buttock
Bend your knee as far as comfortable
Slide back out slowly to straight

compensation_patterns.name: hip_hike
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis tilts during heel slide

regression: Use a towel under the heel to reduce friction

name: Mini Squat
cues: Stand holding back of chair for balance
Bend knees to about 45 degrees â€” like sitting into a shallow chair
Keep knees over toes, not caving inward

compensation_patterns.name: knee_valgus
compensation_patterns.detection: knees collapse inward past the line of the toes

compensation_patterns.name: forward_trunk_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans forward excessively

name: Step-Up
cues: Place entire foot on step, then push through the heel
Stand all the way up on the step â€” fully extend your knee
Lower back down slowly, controlling the descent

compensation_patterns.name: knee_valgus
compensation_patterns.detection: knee collapses inward during step-up

compensation_patterns.name: push_off_trailing_leg
compensation_patterns.detection: trailing leg pushes off floor to assist

name: Step-Down (Forward)
cues: Stand on step, slowly lower opposite foot toward the floor
Keep your stance knee tracking over your second toe
Touch heel lightly to the floor, then return up

compensation_patterns.name: knee_valgus
compensation_patterns.detection: stance knee collapses inward during descent

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lateral_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk shifts laterally over stance leg

name: Terminal Knee Extension
cues: Loop band behind your knee, stand facing the anchor
Start with knee slightly bent, then straighten fully
Squeeze your quad hard at full extension and hold 2 seconds

compensation_patterns.name: hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: knee pushes past 0 degrees into hyperextension

name: Prone Hamstring Curl
cues: Lie face down and bend your knee, bringing heel toward buttock
Squeeze your hamstring at the top
Lower slowly â€” control the weight down

compensation_patterns.name: hip_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: hip lifts off mat during knee flexion

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches excessively during curl

name: Single-Leg Squat to Chair
cues: Stand on one leg in front of a chair
Slowly sit down to the chair, then stand back up on one leg
Keep your knee tracking over your second toe

compensation_patterns.name: knee_valgus
compensation_patterns.detection: knee dives inward past midline of foot

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lateral_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk shifts excessively over stance leg

compensation_patterns.name: contralateral_hip_drop
compensation_patterns.detection: opposite side pelvis drops during squat

regression: Use arms for balance, reduce depth

name: Lateral Band Walk
cues: Place band around ankles, feet shoulder-width apart
Stay in a mini-squat position and step sideways
Keep tension on the band â€” don't let feet come together

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lateral_sway
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk shifts laterally with each step

compensation_patterns.name: toe_out_walking
compensation_patterns.detection: feet externally rotate during side-stepping

regression: Place band above knees instead of ankles
progression: Use heavier band, deeper squat position

name: Wall Sit
cues: Lean against wall and slide down until thighs approach parallel
Keep your knees over your ankles, not past your toes
Press your low back into the wall

compensation_patterns.name: knee_forward_drift
compensation_patterns.detection: knees move anterior past the toes

compensation_patterns.name: asymmetric_loading
compensation_patterns.detection: body shifts weight to one leg

regression: Higher position (less knee bend), shorter hold

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
cues: Place rear foot on bench behind you, laces down
Lower straight down until front thigh is parallel to floor
Push through front heel to stand back up

compensation_patterns.name: knee_valgus
compensation_patterns.detection: front knee collapses inward during descent

compensation_patterns.name: forward_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans forward excessively

name: Clamshell (Knee Focus)
cues: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to 45 degrees
Keeping feet together, lift your top knee toward ceiling
Don't let your pelvis roll backward

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_roll
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis rotates backward during hip opening

name: Seated Knee Extension
cues: Sit tall in a chair, straighten your knee fully
Hold at the top for 2 seconds, squeezing your quad
Lower slowly with control

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lean_back
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans backward to assist extension

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
cues: Kneel with ankles anchored, body tall from knees to head
Slowly lean forward, controlling descent with hamstrings
Catch yourself with hands and push back up to start

compensation_patterns.name: hip_flexion_break
compensation_patterns.detection: hips bend forward instead of staying straight

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches during descent

regression: Use a band for assistance, reduce descent range
progression: Increase eccentric range, add weight vest

name: Prone Knee Flexion ROM
cues: Lie face down and slowly bend your knee, heel toward buttock
Use a strap around ankle to assist if needed
Go to comfortable end range and hold briefly

compensation_patterns.name: hip_hiking
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis lifts off mat on the bending side

name: Standing Hamstring Curl
cues: Hold chair for balance, bend knee bringing heel to buttock
Keep thighs parallel â€” don't let knee drift forward
Lower slowly with control

compensation_patterns.name: hip_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: thigh moves forward during curl

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_forward_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk tilts forward during curl

name: Clamshell
cues: Lie on side with knees bent and feet together
Open your top knee like a clamshell without rolling your pelvis
Slowly close with control

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_roll
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis rolls backward during knee lift

compensation_patterns.name: foot_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: top foot separates from bottom foot

name: Glute Bridge
cues: Lie on back with knees bent and feet flat
Push through heels to lift hips until body forms a straight line
Squeeze glutes at the top and hold 2 seconds

compensation_patterns.name: hamstring_dominance
compensation_patterns.detection: cramping in hamstrings indicates glute underactivation

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: hips lift too high causing low back arch

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
cues: Extend one leg straight, push through the planted heel
Lift hips until body is straight from shoulders to knee
Keep pelvis level â€” don't let the unsupported side drop

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_drop
compensation_patterns.detection: unsupported side of pelvis drops during bridge

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: excessive arching in low back at top of bridge

progression: Add weight across hips, increase hold time

name: Half-Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
cues: Kneel on one knee with the other foot forward
Gently shift hips forward until you feel a stretch in front of the back hip
Keep your trunk upright â€” do not lean forward

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches instead of hip extending

compensation_patterns.name: anterior_pelvic_tilt
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis tilts forward excessively

regression: Place hands on chair for support, less lunge depth
progression: Add overhead reach to increase stretch

name: Fire Hydrant
cues: Start on hands and knees, keep knee bent at 90 degrees
Lift knee out to the side like a dog at a fire hydrant
Keep your hips level â€” don't shift to the opposite side

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lateral_shift
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk shifts away from lifting leg

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: spine rotates during hip abduction

name: Hip Lateral Band Walk
cues: Band around ankles, mini-squat position
Step sideways leading with your heel
Maintain constant tension on the band â€” never let it go slack

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_sway
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans side to side with each step

compensation_patterns.name: knee_valgus
compensation_patterns.detection: knees cave inward during walking

name: Hip Hinge (RDL Pattern)
cues: Hold dowel along spine â€” contact at head, upper back, and tailbone
Push hips straight back while keeping spine neutral
Stand by squeezing glutes to drive hips forward

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: low back rounds â€” dowel loses contact at tailbone

compensation_patterns.name: knee_forward_drift
compensation_patterns.detection: knees bend excessively, converting hinge to squat

regression: Reduce range, touch wall behind with hips

name: Sidelying Hip Abduction
cues: Lie on your side with legs straight and stacked
Lift top leg toward ceiling, leading with heel
Keep toes pointed forward or slightly down â€” do not rotate outward

compensation_patterns.name: hip_flexion_substitution
compensation_patterns.detection: leg drifts forward during abduction

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_roll
compensation_patterns.detection: body rolls backward

name: Prone Hip Extension
cues: Lie face down with legs straight
Squeeze your glute and lift one leg off the mat
Keep your pelvis pressed into the mat â€” no rocking

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches excessively during leg lift

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis rotates during hip extension

regression: Bend knee to 90 degrees, then extend hip

name: Pigeon Stretch
cues: From hands and knees, bring one knee forward and lay shin across the mat
Extend the other leg straight behind you
Lower your body forward over the front leg until you feel a deep stretch

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_tilt
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis tilts to one side instead of staying square

progression: Lean further forward, increase hold to 60 seconds

name: 90/90 Hip Position
cues: Sit with both knees bent at 90 degrees â€” one in front, one to the side
Sit tall and rotate between internal and external rotation positions
Try to keep both sit bones on the floor

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans heavily to one side for support

progression: Add forward lean to increase stretch

name: Copenhagen Adduction
cues: Side plank with top leg on bench, bottom leg hanging
Lift bottom leg up to meet the bench
Keep body in a straight line â€” don't let hips sag

compensation_patterns.name: hip_sag
compensation_patterns.detection: hips drop below the line of shoulders and feet

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk rotates forward or backward during hold

regression: Bend bottom knee, shorter hold, use short lever
progression: Full lever with straight legs, add pulses

name: Standing Hip Flexion
cues: Stand tall and lift your knee toward your chest
Keep your trunk upright â€” don't lean backward
Lower slowly with control

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lean_back
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans backward during hip flexion

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: low back rounds during standing hip flexion

name: Seated Hip Internal Rotation
cues: Sit on a chair with knees bent 90 degrees
Keeping your knee still, swing your foot outward (rotating hip inward)
Hold at end range, feeling the stretch in outer hip

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: same-side buttock lifts off chair

name: Supine Figure-4 Stretch
cues: Lie on back, cross one ankle over opposite knee
Pull the uncrossed leg toward your chest
Keep your low back flat on the mat

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: low back rounds off mat during pull

name: Standing Hip Circles
cues: Stand on one leg, holding support if needed
Draw large circles with your knee, going forward, out, back, and in
Reverse direction after each set

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_sway
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk sways excessively during circles

name: Dead Bug
cues: Lie on back with arms toward ceiling, hips and knees at 90 degrees
Lower opposite arm and leg slowly toward the floor
Press your low back flat into the mat â€” it should not arch

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches off mat during arm/leg extension

compensation_patterns.name: rib_flare
compensation_patterns.detection: ribcage flares upward as arms extend overhead

regression: Move only arms or only legs, not both
progression: Hold light dumbbell, add ankle weight

name: Bird Dog
cues: Start on hands and knees, spine in neutral
Extend opposite arm and leg until parallel with the floor
Hold for 3 seconds â€” no rotation in your trunk

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back sags below neutral during leg extension

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk rotates when lifting arm/leg

regression: Lift only arm or only leg
progression: Add ankle weight, increase hold time

name: Prone Press-Up (McKenzie Extension)
cues: Lie face down, hands under shoulders
Press up straightening elbows while keeping hips on the mat
Let your low back sag â€” do not engage your glutes

compensation_patterns.name: hip_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: hips lift off the mat during press-up

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_shrug
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders elevate toward ears during press-up

regression: Press up on forearms only (sphinx position)
progression: Full elbow extension, sustained hold at top

name: Cat-Cow
cues: Start on hands and knees, spine neutral
Cat: Round your back toward ceiling, tuck chin to chest
Cow: Drop belly toward floor, lift head and tailbone

compensation_patterns.name: cervical_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: neck extends excessively during cow position

progression: Add segmental control â€” move one vertebra at a time

name: Supine Pelvic Tilt
cues: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat
Flatten your low back into the mat by tilting your pelvis
Hold for 5 seconds, then relax

compensation_patterns.name: breath_holding
compensation_patterns.detection: visible apnea during hold â€” chest stops moving

name: McGill Curl-Up
cues: Lie on back, one knee bent, one leg straight, hands under low back
Lift head and shoulders just barely off the mat
Hold for 10 seconds â€” your low back should not move

compensation_patterns.name: neck_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: chin tucks excessively toward chest

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: low back flattens or flexes during curl-up

name: Side Plank (McGill)
cues: Lie on your side, prop up on forearm with elbow under shoulder
Lift hips off the mat â€” body should form a straight line
Hold without letting hips sag or pike up

compensation_patterns.name: hip_sag
compensation_patterns.detection: hips drop below the shoulder-ankle line

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk rotates forward or backward during hold

regression: Bend knees, prop from knees instead of feet
progression: Add top leg lift, increase hold to 30 seconds

name: Pallof Press
cues: Stand sideways to anchor with band at chest height
Press band straight out from chest â€” resist rotation
Return to chest slowly, fighting the pull the entire time

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk rotates toward the anchor during press

compensation_patterns.name: lateral_lean
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk leans away from anchor instead of staying upright

regression: Stand closer to anchor, lighter band
progression: Heavier band, stand further from anchor

name: Supine Hook-Lying Marches
cues: Lie on back with knees bent, flatten low back into mat
Lift one knee toward chest while maintaining pelvic tilt
Lower slowly, then repeat on other side

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches off mat during leg lift

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis rocks side to side during marching

regression: Smaller range of motion, slower tempo

name: Bridge (Lumbar Stabilization)
cues: Lie on back with knees bent, engage core first
Push through heels to lift hips â€” stop when body is straight
Do not arch beyond neutral â€” think straight line from shoulders to knees

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: hips push too high, causing low back arch

name: Forearm Plank
cues: Prop on forearms and toes, body straight as a board
Engage core by pulling belly button toward spine
Don't let your hips sag or pike up

compensation_patterns.name: hip_sag
compensation_patterns.detection: hips drop below the shoulder-ankle line

compensation_patterns.name: hip_pike
compensation_patterns.detection: hips lift above the shoulder-ankle line

regression: From knees instead of toes, shorter hold
progression: Increase hold to 60 seconds, add arm reach

name: Modified Superman
cues: Lie face down with arms extended overhead
Lift opposite arm and leg 2-3 inches off the mat
Hold for 3 seconds, then switch sides

compensation_patterns.name: excessive_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: body lifts too high causing lumbar hyperextension

compensation_patterns.name: neck_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: head lifts excessively looking upward

regression: Lift only arm or only leg
progression: Lift both arms and both legs simultaneously

name: Seated Trunk Rotation
cues: Sit tall with feet flat on the floor
Rotate your trunk to one side, looking over your shoulder
Keep your pelvis facing forward â€” only your trunk rotates

compensation_patterns.name: pelvis_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis rotates with trunk instead of staying fixed

name: Child's Pose
cues: Kneel and sit back on your heels
Reach arms forward and lower your chest toward the floor
Breathe deeply and relax into the stretch

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_impingement
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders shrug toward ears during reach

regression: Place a pillow between thighs and calves
progression: Walk hands to one side for lateral stretch

name: Supine Knee to Chest
cues: Lie on back and pull one knee toward your chest
Hold behind the knee, not on the kneecap
Keep the other leg flat on the mat

compensation_patterns.name: opposite_leg_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: straight leg lifts off mat during pull

progression: Pull both knees to chest simultaneously

name: Supine Lumbar Rotation Stretch
cues: Lie on back with knees bent together
Drop both knees to one side while keeping shoulders flat
Hold for 20 seconds, then switch sides

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: opposite shoulder lifts off mat during rotation

regression: Reduce rotation range, add pillow between knees
progression: Straighten top leg for greater stretch

name: Chin Tuck
cues: Sit or stand tall, looking straight ahead
Gently draw your chin straight back, making a double chin
Hold for 10 seconds â€” don't tilt your head up or down

compensation_patterns.name: head_tilt
compensation_patterns.detection: head tilts downward instead of retracting horizontally

compensation_patterns.name: jaw_protrusion
compensation_patterns.detection: jaw juts forward instead of head retracting

progression: Add manual overpressure with fingertips on chin

name: Deep Neck Flexor Endurance Hold
cues: Lie on your back, tuck chin gently
Lift head just 1 inch off the mat â€” barely off
Hold for 20 seconds without letting chin poke forward

compensation_patterns.name: superficial_flexor_dominance
compensation_patterns.detection: SCM muscles visibly bulge, head lifts too high

compensation_patterns.name: chin_poke
compensation_patterns.detection: chin extends forward losing the tuck position

regression: Shorter hold (10s), head on folded towel

name: Cervical Rotation ROM
cues: Sit tall, slowly turn your head to look over one shoulder
Go as far as comfortable â€” no forcing
Return to center and turn to the other side

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders and trunk rotate instead of just the neck

compensation_patterns.name: lateral_flexion_substitution
compensation_patterns.detection: head tilts sideways instead of rotating

progression: Add gentle overpressure at end range

name: Levator Scapulae Stretch
cues: Sit tall, rotate head 45 degrees to one side
Look down toward your armpit on that side
Use your hand to gently increase the stretch â€” hold 30 seconds

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: opposite shoulder lifts toward ear during stretch

progression: Anchor same-side hand under chair for more stretch

name: Upper Trapezius Stretch
cues: Sit tall, tilt your ear toward your shoulder
Keep the opposite shoulder down â€” anchor it by holding the chair seat
Use your hand to gently increase the stretch

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: stretch-side shoulder rises toward ear

compensation_patterns.name: trunk_lateral_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk bends sideways instead of only neck

regression: Gentle tilt only, no hand pressure

name: Seated Thoracic Extension
cues: Sit tall in a chair with hands behind your head
Gently extend upper back over the chair back
Keep your low back still â€” only the upper back moves

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back extends instead of thoracic spine

compensation_patterns.name: cervical_hyperextension
compensation_patterns.detection: neck extends excessively during movement

progression: Use foam roller for thoracic extension

name: Scapular Retraction (Cervical Focus)
cues: Sit or stand tall with arms at sides
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and down
Hold for 5 seconds â€” think of putting shoulder blades in your back pockets

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders shrug upward during retraction

compensation_patterns.name: cervical_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: head juts forward during squeeze

name: Wall Angels (Cervical Focus)
cues: Stand with back of head, upper back, and buttocks against wall
Maintain chin tuck while sliding arms up and down
Keep wrists and elbows against the wall throughout

compensation_patterns.name: head_protrusion
compensation_patterns.detection: head loses contact with wall during arm movement

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches away from wall

name: Prone Cervical Retraction
cues: Lie face down with forehead on a folded towel
Gently tuck chin and lift head 1 inch off towel
Hold for 5 seconds â€” keep your neck long and chin tucked

compensation_patterns.name: cervical_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: neck extends, head lifts too high looking forward

name: Isometric Cervical Multiplanar
cues: Place hand on forehead, push head into hand without moving
Repeat with hand on each side and on back of head
Use 50% effort â€” hold 6 seconds each direction

compensation_patterns.name: visible_movement
compensation_patterns.detection: head moves against hand instead of isometric hold

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulders shrug upward during isometric

name: Cervical Flexion ROM
cues: Sit tall, slowly lower your chin toward your chest
Go as far as comfortable, feeling a gentle stretch in the back of your neck
Return slowly to neutral

compensation_patterns.name: thoracic_flexion
compensation_patterns.detection: upper back rounds forward during neck flexion

name: Cervical Lateral Flexion ROM
cues: Sit tall, tilt your ear toward your shoulder
Keep your nose pointing forward â€” don't rotate
Return to center and repeat on the other side

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: shoulder lifts toward the ear instead of ear dropping to shoulder

name: Cervical Extension ROM
cues: Sit tall, slowly look up toward the ceiling
Move gently â€” stop if you feel dizziness
Return to neutral slowly

compensation_patterns.name: lumbar_extension
compensation_patterns.detection: low back arches during neck extension

regression: Reduce range, use hand for support

name: Scalene Stretch
cues: Sit tall, grip chair seat with one hand to anchor shoulder
Tilt head away and slightly rotate toward the stretch side
Hold for 30 seconds â€” you should feel a stretch on the side of your neck

compensation_patterns.name: shoulder_elevation
compensation_patterns.detection: stretch-side shoulder rises during stretch

progression: Increase hold to 45 seconds, add slight extension

name: Cervical Retraction with Extension
cues: First perform a chin tuck (retraction)
While maintaining the retraction, look up toward the ceiling
Return to neutral, then repeat

compensation_patterns.name: loss_of_retraction
compensation_patterns.detection: chin pokes forward as head extends

progression: Add manual overpressure at end range

name: Ankle Alphabet
cues: Sit with leg elevated or hanging off edge of bed
Trace the alphabet with your big toe â€” A through Z
Move only from your ankle, keep your leg still

compensation_patterns.name: knee_movement
compensation_patterns.detection: knee moves instead of ankle during tracing

name: Wall Dorsiflexion Stretch
cues: Stand facing wall with toes a few inches from the wall
Bend knee forward to touch wall, keeping heel on the ground
If easy, move foot further from wall and repeat

compensation_patterns.name: heel_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: heel lifts off ground during knee drive

compensation_patterns.name: foot_pronation
compensation_patterns.detection: arch collapses inward during stretch

progression: Move foot further from wall, add knee-to-wall measurement

name: Eccentric Heel Drop (Straight Knee - Alfredson)
cues: Stand on the edge of a step on the ball of your affected foot
Rise up on toes using both feet, then lift the unaffected foot
Slowly lower the affected heel below step level over 5 seconds

compensation_patterns.name: knee_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: knee flexes during the heel drop

compensation_patterns.name: rapid_descent
compensation_patterns.detection: heel drops too quickly, less than 3 seconds

regression: Use both legs, reduce range below step
progression: Add backpack weight, increase sets to 6

name: Eccentric Heel Drop (Bent Knee - Alfredson)
cues: Stand on step edge, bend your knee about 30 degrees
Rise up on toes with both feet, then lift unaffected foot
Slowly lower heel below step â€” keep the knee bent throughout

compensation_patterns.name: knee_straightening
compensation_patterns.detection: knee extends during the descent

name: Standing Calf Raise
cues: Stand near wall for balance, feet hip-width apart
Rise up onto toes as high as possible
Lower slowly with control â€” full 3-second descent

compensation_patterns.name: knee_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: knees flex during the raise

compensation_patterns.name: ankle_inversion
compensation_patterns.detection: weight shifts to outside of foot during raise

name: Seated Calf Raise
cues: Sit with feet flat on the floor, knees bent 90 degrees
Push up onto your toes, lifting your heels off the floor
Lower slowly â€” this targets the soleus muscle specifically

compensation_patterns.name: ankle_inversion
compensation_patterns.detection: feet roll outward during the raise

progression: Place weight on knees (dumbbell or plate)

name: Single-Leg Balance
cues: Stand on one foot near a wall for safety
Keep your knee slightly soft, not locked
Focus on a spot on the wall for balance

compensation_patterns.name: excessive_trunk_sway
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk sways more than 5cm from midline

compensation_patterns.name: hip_strategy_dominant
compensation_patterns.detection: large hip movements used instead of ankle strategy

regression: Touch wall with fingertip, hold for 15 seconds
progression: Close eyes, stand on foam pad

name: Single-Leg Balance Eyes Closed
cues: Stand on one foot near a wall for safety
Once balanced, close your eyes
Try to hold for 15 seconds â€” touch wall if you lose balance

compensation_patterns.name: excessive_trunk_sway
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk sways more than 5cm from midline

progression: Stand on foam pad with eyes closed

name: BAPS Board (Wobble Board) Balance
cues: Stand on wobble board with both feet
Slowly rock the board clockwise in a circle
Try to keep the edges from touching the floor

compensation_patterns.name: excessive_trunk_movement
compensation_patterns.detection: trunk moves excessively instead of controlling from ankle

regression: Hold onto support, seated wobble board
progression: Single-leg wobble board, add ball catch

name: Peroneal Strengthening with Band (Eversion)
cues: Sit with leg extended, band around forefoot anchored to inside
Push foot outward (eversion) against the band
Return slowly â€” control the motion both ways

compensation_patterns.name: knee_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: knee rotates outward instead of ankle everting

name: Tibialis Posterior Strengthening (Inversion)
cues: Sit with leg extended, band around forefoot anchored to outside
Pull foot inward (inversion) against the band
Return slowly with control

compensation_patterns.name: knee_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: knee rotates inward instead of ankle inverting

name: Toe Curls (Towel Scrunch)
cues: Place a towel flat on the floor, foot on top
Scrunch the towel toward you by curling your toes
Spread toes between reps

compensation_patterns.name: ankle_plantarflexion
compensation_patterns.detection: ankle points downward instead of toes curling

progression: Place a weight on the towel

name: Resisted Ankle Eversion
cues: Tie band to a fixed object, loop around outside of foot
Push foot outward against band resistance
Keep knee still â€” only your ankle should move

compensation_patterns.name: hip_abduction_substitution
compensation_patterns.detection: entire leg moves outward instead of ankle

progression: Heavier band, add single-leg stance eversion

name: Resisted Ankle Inversion
cues: Tie band to a fixed object, loop around inside of foot
Pull foot inward against band resistance
Keep knee still â€” isolate the ankle movement

compensation_patterns.name: hip_adduction_substitution
compensation_patterns.detection: entire leg moves inward instead of ankle

name: Standing Calf Stretch (Gastrocnemius)
cues: Step one foot back, keep back knee straight and heel on the ground
Lean into the wall until you feel a stretch in the back calf
Hold for 30 seconds

compensation_patterns.name: heel_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: back heel lifts off floor during stretch

compensation_patterns.name: foot_external_rotation
compensation_patterns.detection: back foot turns outward to cheat the stretch

progression: Longer stance, deeper lean, elevated forefoot

name: Standing Soleus Stretch (Bent Knee)
cues: Step one foot back, bend the back knee while keeping heel down
Lean into the wall â€” you should feel a deeper, lower calf stretch
Hold for 30 seconds

compensation_patterns.name: heel_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: heel lifts off ground during stretch

name: Banded Ankle Dorsiflexion Mobilization
cues: Loop heavy band low around front of ankle, anchored behind you
In a half-kneeling position, drive knee forward over toes
The band pulls the talus backward â€” improving joint mobility

compensation_patterns.name: heel_lift
compensation_patterns.detection: heel lifts off the ground during knee drive

compensation_patterns.name: foot_pronation
compensation_patterns.detection: foot arch collapses during mobilization

progression: Heavier band, greater knee forward travel

name: Tibialis Anterior Raise
cues: Lean your back against a wall with feet about a foot away
Lift your toes and forefeet off the ground toward your shins
Lower slowly â€” control the descent

compensation_patterns.name: knee_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: knees flex to assist the dorsiflexion

name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
cues: Stand on one foot on the edge of a step
Rise up onto your toes as high as possible
Lower slowly below the step level for full range

compensation_patterns.name: knee_bend
compensation_patterns.detection: knee flexes during the raise

compensation_patterns.name: hip_hike
compensation_patterns.detection: pelvis tilts to assist the raise

regression: Use wall for balance, bilateral calf raise

name: Active Ankle Dorsiflexion
cues: Sit with leg extended, pull your toes toward your shin
Hold at end range for 2 seconds
Relax and repeat â€” focus on full available range

compensation_patterns.name: toe_curling
compensation_patterns.detection: toes curl instead of pointing upward

name: Ankle Circles
cues: Sit or lie with foot elevated
Draw slow, controlled circles with your foot
Make the circles as large as pain allows, then reverse direction

compensation_patterns.name: knee_movement
compensation_patterns.detection: knee moves in circles instead of ankle
